content.full_text:
COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
OFFICE OF THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY
18000 CRIMINAL COURTS BUILDING
210 WEST TEMPLE STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012
974-3501

JOHN K VAN DE KAMP DISTRICT ATTORNEY

July 20, 1976

Mr. Delbert Spurlock, Chief
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

This letter is written to memorialize a telephone conference
of July 9, 1976, wherein representatives of my office dis-
cussed certain questions with Ms. Marilyn Aguirre and
Ms. Dawn Weiser of your staff. Ms. Weiser and Ms. Aguirre
were very generous of their time and were explicit and
direct in their responses to our inquiries. We are very
much obliged to them and to you for their assistance. The
following is an encapsulated statement of the questions and
answers given:

1) Question: When is the first report due from a
District Attorney who was appointed on October 14,
1975, to fill the unexpired term of an elected
District Attorney who took office in December, 1972,
that appointed District Attorney having been
elected in June, 1976, to commence a new term
beginning on December 6, 1976?

Answer: Thirty days after the commencement of the
term to which he is elected, i.e., on 5 January,
1977.

2) Question: Where is the District Attorney's conflict
of interest disclosure form to be filed?

Answer: The instructions contained in the F.P.P.C.
memorandum to District Attorneys of 26 May, 1976, is
to be followed. Conflicting instructions in the
sample forms 720 and 721 accompanying this memo are


Mr. Delbert Spurlock
Page 2
July 20, 1976

to be ignored. Therefore the original disclosure
form is to be sent by the District Attorney's
Office to the Fair Political Practices Commission
and a copy retained by the District Attorney's
Office. A copy need not be filed with the County
Clerk.

3) Question: Given the conditions expressed in
question one, which form is to be filed in January,
1977, form 720 or form 721?

Answer: 721 only.

4) Question: What period, if any, must be covered in
reporting gifts and income in form 721?

Answer: The period beginning May 5, 1976, and
ending December 6, 1976. This is different from
the requirement imposed on those who report pursuant
to a locally adopted conflict of interest code who
need not disclose their income and gifts for the
year 1976. Disclosure of income and gifts for those
persons must be made beginning with the first
anniversary report.

5) Question: Are reimbursements received from agencies
other than state and local government for duty
related travel, lodging, meals, and incidental
expenses required to be reported?

Answer: Yes, as the law is presently written.

If our understanding of any of the above is incorrect, I would
appreciate being advised.

Thank you again for your courteous cooperation in this matter.

Sincerely,

JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP
District Attorney

mw


Prime /

FPDS TRANSMITTER OF CORRESPONDENCE

CORRESPONDENT: John Van de Kamp

DATE 7/23                    RE: disclosure questions

COPY TO:

ADMINISTRATOR
[illegible]
BOG T.
[illegible]
J.D.
JUST

ENFORCEMENT          CONFIDENTIAL          NOR S.
                    [circled: DEL]         MICK
                    DO
                    [illegible]
                    [illegible]
                    [illegible]

EXECUTIVE                LEGAL                TASK
DEM                       MAN F.               JOHN K.
[illegible]             [illegible]
CARDA                    [illegible]          A 76-432
                         RED
                         [illegible]


                         Shawn
                         Marilyn
                         No Response Pending
                    ilel        Process: A-OK


Take whatever action you consider appropriate.  Early direct b. 7/30 -- cc: [illegible]
prepare suggested reply and return to
Coordinate reply w/[illegible]

[illegible]

[illegible]
